turns.user_message: I was just sitting on the couch earlier today, watching the light fade through the window, and it struck me how quiet everything felt. We were in the same room, but the air between us felt completely heavy and thick. I tried to make a casual joke about the weather, but it just bounced right off the wall. Now I am just staring at the ceiling wondering why such a simple moment felt so incredibly draining. I keep replaying the way the silence stretched out, and it leaves me feeling completely hollow.

That heavy silence from earlier has been creeping into almost every evening lately, turning what used to be comfortable routines into something exhausting. I find myself carefully measuring my words just to avoid triggering another round of that same cold distance. It feels like I am walking on eggshells in my own living room, constantly second-guessing every little thing I say. The more I try to bridge the gap, the wider it seems to get, leaving me completely drained. I just wish I could remember what it felt like to share a space without feeling so completely alone.

Last night, sitting in that exact same spot on the couch, that familiar quiet finally broke, but it only made everything feel so much worse. I watched them sit across from me, completely checked out, and realized I have no idea who I am talking to anymore. Every attempt to reach out just hits a wall of indifference, and it is starting to eat away at my sense of reality. I am so tired of pretending that this hollow routine is enough, but letting go feels terrifyingly impossible. I just feel completely trapped in a loop of empty gestures and unspoken resentment.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This has been going on for a while.
